sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The LEFT action didn't move the blocks. They're still at cols 49-53, rows 20-21. So the blocks can't go LEFT from this position.
Let me check the area around the blocks to understand the maze structure better.
Looking at the area:
- Rows 17-19: GGGGGGGGccccc (gray passage to the left, charcoal to the right)
- Rows 20-21: cccOOOOOGGGGG (blocks at cols 49-53, gray to the right)
- Rows 22-24: cccbbbbbGGGGG (blue below blocks, gray to the right)
The blocks are at cols 49-53. To the left (cols 46-48) is charcoal (ccc). So the blocks can't go LEFT.
But looking at rows 17-19, the area shows "GGGGGGGGccccc". The "GGGGGGGG" is at cols 46-53 (gray), and "ccccc" is at cols 54-58 (charcoal). So the passage at rows 17-19 is at cols 46-53 (gray).
This means the blocks at cols 49-53, rows 20-21 can go UP to rows 17-19, where the passage is wider (cols 46-53). From there, the blocks can go LEFT.
Let me try going UP from cols 49-53, rows 20-21 to reach rows 17-19, then LEFT.
But the game ended after 2 UP actions from cols 49-53, rows 20-21. The blocks moved to cols 49-53, rows 10-11. This is 10 rows up, not 3 rows up.
Wait, let me re-examine. The blocks were at cols 49-53, rows 20-21. After 2 UP actions, they moved to cols 49-53, rows 10-11. That's 10 rows up, which is 5 rows per UP action.
So from cols 49-53, 1 UP → 5 rows up. The blocks moved from rows 20-21 to rows 15-16 after 1 UP, and from rows 15-16 to rows 10-11 after 2 UP.
But the game ended after 2 UP actions. This is strange. Let me check if the game ended because the blocks reached rows 10-11, or because of some other reason.
Actually, looking at the earlier output, the game ended after 2 UP actions from cols 49-53, rows 20-21. The blocks moved to cols 49-53, rows 10-11. But the level was not completed.
Let me try going UP 1 action from cols 49-53, rows 20-21 to reach rows 15-16, then LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks at cols 49-53, rows 20-21
# Try UP 1 to reach rows 15-16, then LEFT

# UP 1
result = action(['UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After UP - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break
print("Game over:", result.get('game_over', False))

# LEFT 1
if not result.get('game_over', False):
    result2 = action(['LEFT'])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'O':
            print(f"After LEFT - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
            break
    print("Game over:", result2.get('game_over', False))
    print("Level completed:", result2.get('level_completed', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
